reason: The one-shot v7 preflight passed both no-model authentication bootstraps and the Claude Opus, Claude Sonnet, and Codex Sol provider handshakes. Codex Luna at medium reasoning then reached the frozen 900-second provider timeout; the evaluator quiesced the original provider process group, re-attested the committed Codex credential link, and rejected the nonzero provider result. The fail-fast v7 preflight returned at that point, so neither Cursor profile was invoked. V7 reported no score and started no production assignment. It is preserved rather than reset or retried. V8 requires a fresh cohort, authentication key, private schedule, and credential namespaces; an exact 1800-second timeout; explicit Sol-medium and Luna-max reasoning; bounded content-free progress telemetry; safe timeout classification; and continuation to independent later profiles after cleanly contained ordinary failures.
